title: Hot and Sour Soup

seo.title: Authentic Hot and Sour Soup Recipe | Easy Chinese Soup
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with our easy, authentic recipe. Learn how to make this classic spicy and tangy soup at home. Perfect for a comforting meal!

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A steaming bowl of authentic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots.
seo.imageCaption: Warm up with a bowl of this classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup, bursting with spicy and tangy flavors.

description: Experience the vibrant flavors of a classic Chinese Hot and Sour Soup, a delightful balance of savory, spicy, and tangy notes. This comforting soup, packed with mushrooms, tofu, and bamboo shoots, is a staple in Chinese cuisine. Perfect for a chilly evening or as a flavorful appetizer, it's a culinary journey in every spoonful.

ingredients.notes: mixed with 4 tbsp cold water for slurry

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Slice the chicken or pork thinly. Dice the firm tofu into small strips. If using dried mushrooms, rehydrate them according to package instructions, then slice thinly. Prepare the bamboo shoots and wood ear mushrooms.

instructions.title: Cook Meat and Mushrooms
instructions.text: In a large pot, bring the chicken broth to a boil. Add the sliced meat, shiitake mushrooms, wood ear mushrooms, and bamboo shoots. Reduce heat and simmer for 5 minutes until the meat is cooked through.

instructions.title: Add Tofu and Seasonings
instructions.text: Stir in the diced tofu, rice vinegar, soy sauce, and white pepper. Taste and adjust the vinegar and soy sauce as needed to achieve your desired hot and sour balance.

instructions.title: Thicken the Soup
instructions.text: Give the cornstarch slurry a quick stir and slowly pour it into the simmering soup while continuously stirring. Continue to stir until the soup thickens to your preferred consistency. This should take about 1-2 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Egg Ribbons
instructions.text: Slowly drizzle the beaten eggs into the hot soup in a thin stream, stirring gently in one direction with chopsticks or a fork to create delicate egg ribbons. Do not stir vigorously.

instructions.title: Finish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the pot from heat. Stir in the sesame oil and chili oil. Ladle the hot and sour soup into individual bowls and garnish generously with chopped green onions before serving immediately.

tips: For the best egg ribbons, ensure your soup is simmering but not boiling vigorously when you drizzle in the beaten eggs. Stirring gently in one direction helps create fine strands.
Adjust the level of spiciness and sourness to your liking. Add more white pepper for heat, chili oil for a deeper spice, and rice vinegar for extra tang.
If you prefer a vegetarian version, omit the meat and use vegetable broth. You can also add other vegetables like carrots or water chestnuts for more texture.
article: Hot and Sour Soup is a beloved Chinese Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
